meaning: adj.安静的,稳重的v.给...服镇静剂
examples: Her life was sedate, almost mundane.
We set off again at a more sedate pace.
We followed the youngsters at a more sedate pace.
For something more sedate, the Comb Artspace puts on quality, contemporary exhibitions that belie its compact space.
My view of Britain therefore, not surprisingly, was of a rather sedate and sophisticated society.
